This page is just for you.

Enter the code to continue.

Made with love.

Vaishnavi, some things stay.

A page I made, because some feelings don't have an expiry date.

scroll down
01 — A Letter

I've been thinking about us, Vaishnavi.

Not in the dramatic, can't-sleep way. More like a quiet background hum — the kind you only notice when everything else goes silent.

I know I came late with my clarity. Late with the courage to say what you meant to me. Late with the patience you deserved. But not late in love — never late in love. That part has always been certain.

This isn't a grand gesture. It's just me, being honest one more time.

The way you fit right here — that's what I keep thinking about.

02 — Us, in Pictures

Some photos just hit different.

The way you held on.
Your smile. My weakness.
Mirror moments.
That night. That look.
Flowers for you. Always.
Home was wherever you were.
Every arrival felt like coming home.
03 — Things I Remember

Small moments that stayed with me.

Not the big dates or the planned outings. The real stuff. The in-between moments when we weren't trying to be anything — we just were.

Us & Leo
Our little family moment. Leo loved you — and honestly, so did I.
He Chose You Too
The way Leo just climbed into your lap. He knew. Animals always know.
Date Nights
Those evening walks, those little cafés. With you, even the ordinary felt extraordinary.
Adventures
Exploring palaces and new cities. Every trip with you felt like we were building a world only we knew.
The prettiest girl in every room. And she never even tried.

Our little family.

You, me, and Leo. That was the whole world, Vaishnavi.

04 — Words I Wrote for You

A poem for Vaishnavi.

I never meant to write another chapter about us,

but every apology still tastes like the way I lost you —

a quiet storm I created with my own hands.

Mistakes that should've never been made,

words that should've never been left unsaid.

And yet, here I am —

still asking for forgiveness,

still hoping you'll hear the truth behind every tremble in my voice.

Some days it feels like I'm learning to breathe again.

Other days, like today,

I'm the moon stuck in its own orbit —

trying to move forward,

but circling the same memory of you.

A friend asked me how you've been,

and my heart stumbled,

as if it couldn't understand how the world

keeps using your name so easily

while mine cracks every time it tries to say it.

I know I came late with my clarity,

late with the courage to articulate what you meant,

late with the patience you deserved.

But not late in love —

never late in love.

That part has always been certain.

I've tried to show you

how we could be better,

how we could rebuild,

how our 'almost' could still become

the kind of love that stays.

Not perfect —

but permanent.

I'm still the man who will hold your hand

and never let you fall —

but I can only do that

if you let my fingers find yours again.

Right now,

I'm drowning in the silence we've built,

reaching for your hand in the dark,

hoping you'll reach back —

not because you owe me,

but because somewhere,

a part of you still remembers

the way my heart said your name.

If this is my last poem about you,

then let it be the one

where I tell you with every breath I have —

give me a chance,

and I'll spend the rest of my life showing

that we were meant to get our ending right.

"If you let my fingers find yours again."

05 — Our Story

Every moment with you was a chapter I didn't want to end.

The Playful Ones

You on the grass with Leo, laughing, not caring about anything else. That's the version of happiness I keep replaying.

The Quiet Evenings

When the world outside didn't matter. Just us, just this. Those were the moments that made everything else worth it.

Road Trips & Stolen Glances

You in the passenger seat, sunlight catching your smile. I kept driving just so the moment wouldn't end.

The Simple Ones

No filters, no poses. Just us, exactly as we were. And somehow, that was always more than enough.

06 — A Word For You

A small word, a small letter, always for you.

Nothing to ask for. Nothing to prove. Just a little love left here for you each day. Reveals at 11:00 AM.

07 — A Question

Do you still think about it, Vaishnavi?

You don't have to answer. You being here, reading this, scrolling this far — maybe that's an answer in itself.

Or maybe it's not. Either way, I wanted you to know: you mattered. You still do.

Not perfect — but permanent. That's the kind of love I'm offering. The kind that stays.

You are loved,
more gently, more deeply,
than these words will ever fully hold.

— with all my love, always.

Does She Still Care?
